_source: Machinery's Handbook, CNC Programming Handbook, NIST tolerances, Shop practices
_note: Long-tail machining knowledge. Speeds/feeds calculations, GD&T, tool selection, tolerances, setup procedures.

titanium.note: Use flood coolant; titanium work-hardens and catches fire easily

feed_rates.ipt_chip_load: Inches per tooth; how much material each tooth removes
feed_rates.formula: Feed rate (IPM) = RPM × number of teeth × chip load
feed_rates.example: 4-flute 1/2" carbide end mill in aluminum, 3000 RPM, 0.010 IPT → Feed = 3000 × 4 × 0.010 = 120 IPM

depth_of_cut.roughing: 50-75% of cutter diameter radial; full depth axial (within reason)
depth_of_cut.slotting: 50% cutter diameter max width; full engagement is hardest on tool; reduce feed 50%
depth_of_cut.climb_vs_conventional: Climb milling (down milling) preferred for carbide; cleaner finish, longer tool life; conventional for HSS or loose machines

datum_reference_frame.primary_datum: 3 points of contact; constrains 3 degrees of freedom (typically a plane)
datum_reference_frame.secondary_datum: 2 points of contact; constrains 2 DOF (typically a line perpendicular to primary)
datum_reference_frame.tertiary_datum: 1 point of contact; constrains 1 DOF (rotation about secondary)
datum_reference_frame.order_matters: A-B-C is different from A-C-B; datum order defines how part is fixtured

form_controls.flatness: Surface must lie between two parallel planes separated by tolerance; no datum needed
form_controls.straightness: Line elements must lie between two parallel lines; no datum; used on cylindrical features
form_controls.circularity_roundness: Cross-section must lie between two concentric circles separated by tolerance; no datum
form_controls.cylindricity: Surface must lie between two coaxial cylinders; combines roundness, straightness, taper; no datum

orientation_controls.perpendicularity: Feature must be 90° to datum within tolerance zone; requires datum
orientation_controls.parallelism: Feature must be parallel to datum within tolerance zone
orientation_controls.angularity: Feature at specified angle to datum; tolerance zone defined by two parallel planes

location_controls.position: Most common; defines true position; often uses MMC modifier for bonus tolerance
location_controls.concentricity: Median points of feature must lie within cylindrical tolerance zone centered on datum
location_controls.symmetry: Median points equidistant from datum plane

profile_controls.profile_of_a_line: 2D tolerance zone; controls form of a cross-section

runout_controls.circular_runout: Tolerance at each circular element when rotated about datum axis; like circularity + position
runout_controls.total_runout: Tolerance for entire surface when rotated; like cylindricity + position; tighter control than circular runout

material_condition_modifiers.mmc: Maximum Material Condition; smallest hole or largest shaft; adds bonus tolerance as feature deviates from MMC
material_condition_modifiers.lmc: Least Material Condition; largest hole or smallest shaft; less common
material_condition_modifiers.rfs: Regardless of Feature Size; no bonus tolerance; default if not specified
material_condition_modifiers.example: Hole Ø0.500 ±0.005, position Ø0.010 at MMC; at MMC (Ø0.495), position Ø0.010; at Ø0.505, position Ø0.020 (gains 0.010 bonus)

standard_tolerances.it_grades: International Tolerance Grades; IT01 (tightest) to IT16 (loosest); IT7-IT9 common for machining
standard_tolerances.fractional: ±1/64" typical for fractional dimensions unless otherwise specified

transition_fits.lt1_locational_transition: -0.0002 to +0.0008" (can be slight interference or clearance); location of parts

surface_finish.achieving_finish: Increase SFM, reduce feed, sharper tool, smaller nose radius, coolant, finer grit (grinding)

end_mill_types.corner_radius: Rounded corners; stronger than square; common in roughing
end_mill_types.roughing_hog: Serrated flutes; break chips; high material removal
end_mill_types.finishing: 4-6 flutes; fine finish; lower chip load

carbide_grades.c1_c4: Hardest, most wear resistant; cast iron, hard materials; brittle
carbide_grades.c5_c8: General purpose; steel, stainless; balanced toughness and wear resistance
carbide_grades.c9_c12: Toughest; interrupted cuts, roughing; less wear resistant

coatings.tin: Titanium Nitride; gold color; general purpose; 2-3× tool life
coatings.tialn: Titanium Aluminum Nitride; purple/black; high heat; dry machining
coatings.altin: Aluminum Titanium Nitride; black/gray; very high heat; hardened steel

tool_holding.collets: ER collets most common; 1 mm range per collet; 0.0003-0.0005" TIR typical
tool_holding.end_mill_holders: Set screw (cheap, low TIR), weldon (better), hydraulic (best, 0.0001" TIR, expensive)
tool_holding.toolholder_taper: CAT40, BT40, HSK63; taper holds tool; drawbar pulls tight; clean taper and spindle face critical
tool_holding.runout: Check with indicator; >0.001" TIR causes poor finish, premature wear, broken tools

g_code_fundamentals.g00: Rapid positioning; non-cutting move; machine max speed; use for tool changes, rapid traverse
g_code_fundamentals.g01: Linear interpolation; cutting move; feed rate applied; most common cutting code
g_code_fundamentals.g02: Circular interpolation CW; I, J, K or R format; arcs and circles
g_code_fundamentals.g17_g18_g19: Plane selection; G17 XY (most common), G18 XZ (lathe), G19 YZ
g_code_fundamentals.g20_g21: G20 inch mode; G21 metric mode; set at program start
g_code_fundamentals.g28: Return to home/reference position; often used at end of program
g_code_fundamentals.g40_g41_g42: Cutter comp off/left/right; compensates for cutter diameter; uses D offset
g_code_fundamentals.g43: Tool length compensation; uses H offset; accounts for different tool lengths
g_code_fundamentals.g54_g59: Work coordinate systems; G54 most common; sets part zero
g_code_fundamentals.g80_g89: Canned cycles; drilling, tapping, boring; G80 cancels cycle
g_code_fundamentals.g90_g91: Absolute vs incremental positioning; G90 from part zero, G91 from current position

m_codes.m01: Optional stop; only if optional stop button enabled
m_codes.m02: Program end; spindle/coolant off; often replaced by M30
m_codes.m03_m04_m05: Spindle on CW/CCW/off; S word sets speed
m_codes.m06: Tool change; T word calls tool; machine goes to change position
m_codes.m30: Program end; reset to start; spindle/coolant off; preferred over M02

work_offsets.setting_g54: Touch off on part surface; zero Z; set X and Y to part zero location
work_offsets.multiple_offsets: G54-G59; up to 6 (or more) work coordinate systems; useful for multiple parts or fixtures
work_offsets.tool_length_offset: Touch off on gage or part surface; store in H offset; called with G43 H1 (for T1)

simple_pocket: G17 G20 G40 G49 G80 G90 (safety line)
G43 H1 Z0.1 (tool length comp, rapid to clearance)
G01 Z-0.25 F10. (plunge to depth, 10 IPM)
G28 Z0 M05 (home Z, spindle off)

chatter.causes: Tool stickout too long, dull tool, incorrect speeds/feeds, part not rigid
solutions: Reduce stickout; use shortest tool possible
Increase/decrease RPM to find sweet spot; avoid harmonic frequencies
Reduce depth of cut or width of cut
chatter.surface_finish: Chatter leaves wave pattern; perpendicular to cutting direction

poor_surface_finish.causes: Dull tool, incorrect feeds/speeds, built-up edge, rubbing
solutions: Use coolant to prevent built-up edge (especially aluminum)
Check for tool deflection; reduce overhang
Use climb milling instead of conventional

broken_tools.causes: Feed rate too high, depth of cut too aggressive, chip packing, tool runout
prevention: Use proper chip load for material and tooling
Ensure good chip evacuation; use air or coolant
Check runout; should be <0.001" for end mills
Peck drill deep holes; don't drill full depth at once
